steps.title: Express the total number of animals
steps.content: Let $c$ be the number of cats and $d$ be the number of dogs. The total number of animals cared for is $c+d$.

steps.title: Translate "at most 43" into an inequality
steps.content: The phrase "at most 43" means the total is less than or equal to 43, so $c+d \le 43$.

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
Compare to each option:
  • A) $c-d \le 43$ uses subtraction, not the sum of animals — eliminate.
  • B) $c-d \ge 43$ uses subtraction and the wrong direction — eliminate.
  • C) $c+d \ge 43$ has the correct sum but reverses the direction (this says at least 43) — eliminate.
  • D) $c+d \le 43$ correctly expresses "the total is at most 43."
That matches choice D — D is correct.
